fields.description: We need a federated-learning aggregation server that combines client model updates without seeing individual gradients. Clients are mobile devices that train locally on user data; the server must aggregate via secure-aggregation (homomorphic / additive masking) and apply differential-privacy noise to the global model. Looking for FL frameworks (Flower, FedML, TensorFlow Federated), secure-aggregation libraries, and DP-SGD aggregator implementations.
fields.current_approach:
- Plain FedAvg with no privacy primitives
- Server sees raw gradients, central aggregation
- No DP budget tracking, no Byzantine handling
success_criteria: Server cannot reconstruct any single client's gradient
Differential-privacy budget tracked per training round
Robust to client dropouts mid-round (Byzantine tolerance)
Supports both FedAvg and FedProx aggregation strategies
constraints: Differential-privacy library that's audited (e.g., Google DP, OpenDP)
No Trusted Execution Environment (TEE) requirement — pure crypto
non_goals: Cross-silo enterprise FL (we focus on cross-device)
Model architecture design (we accept whatever the clients train)
